title: Forces Acting on Structures and Mechanisms
unitGoal: Students will identify forces, loads, materials, shapes, and design choices that help structures and mechanisms work safely.

lessons.title: Structures and Forces
lessons.teacherOverview: Planned student activity set on identifying structures, mechanisms, pushes, pulls, loads, and real-life force examples.

lessons.title: Compression, Tension, Torsion, and Shear
lessons.teacherOverview: Planned student activity set on major force types using age-appropriate examples and diagrams.

lessons.title: Loads and Stability
lessons.teacherOverview: Planned student activity set on live loads, dead loads, balance, centre of gravity, and stability.

lessons.title: Materials, Shapes, and Strength
lessons.teacherOverview: Planned student activity set on how material choices, triangles, arches, tubes, and bracing affect strength.

lessons.title: Mechanisms and Motion
lessons.teacherOverview: Planned student activity set on how simple mechanisms transfer force and motion in useful ways.

lessons.title: Designing and Testing a Structure
lessons.teacherOverview: Planned student activity set on designing, testing, improving, and explaining a structure using evidence.

lessons.title: Real-World Structures and Safety
lessons.teacherOverview: Planned student activity set on bridges, towers, buildings, playground equipment, maintenance, and safety.

lessons.title: Unit 4 Final Quiz
lessons.teacherOverview: Planned final quiz on forces, loads, stability, materials, shapes, mechanisms, and structure design choices.
